executive_summary:
• The synthetic demo shows the operating model rather than real business performance: revenue, sold-item COGS, disposed-inventory write-offs, margin, cash effect, and active inventory all roll up from normalized source tables.
  • The important dashboard pattern is separation of operating economics from cash timing. Sold-item COGS and disposed-inventory COGS are visible separately, while inventory/labor cash outflow remains a cash-management signal.
  • The next decision in a real deployment would be prioritizing stale active listings and pending-sale cleanup before expanding sourcing volume.
operational_commentary: This sanitized dashboard is a portfolio/demo version. It demonstrates how an operator can review pipeline health, aged listings, margin outliers, write-offs, and work cues from one static HTML report without exposing private operational data.

section_commentary.pl_cash_trend: The trend view separates receipts, gross margin, and net cash effect so operators can see whether sales quality and cash movement are telling the same story.
section_commentary.stale_status: Stale status rows are the operational queue: each item should either get a status correction, a listing refresh, a buyer follow-up, or a disposal decision.
section_commentary.current_inventory: The inventory pipeline table shows where inventory is sitting now and whether target value is concentrated in items that are actually listed.
section_commentary.profitability: Margin tables are designed to identify both winners worth replicating and low/negative margin transactions that need explanation.
section_commentary.aging_inventory: Aged listing rows should drive markdowns, photo refreshes, bundling, relisting, or disposal decisions rather than sitting passively.
section_commentary.storage_workflow: In the public demo this section is represented through pipeline and cash metrics; private deployments can add storage-unit detail when safe.
section_commentary.partner_balance: Partner-specific balances are intentionally omitted from the public demo unless replaced with synthetic examples.

anomaly_action_cues: Pending-sale hygiene: demo pending items without complete scheduling metadata should be followed up or moved back to active if the buyer is stale.
Listing age: items with high days-since-listed should get a markdown, photo refresh, relist, cross-post, bundle, or disposal decision.
Write-off visibility: disposed inventory appears as separate COGS so operators can distinguish true sold-item economics from operational loss/write-off decisions.
jira_work_cues: Compliance/bookkeeping lane: keep cash-flow taxonomy and receipt-backed expense capture clean before relying on margin reports for decisions.
Listing execution lane: prioritize aged active listings and stale pending-sale rows because those are closest to cash conversion.
Automation maturity lane: keep dashboard generation repeatable with context export, operator-authored analysis, and CI smoke checks.
